item.title: 🇺🇦 Russians panic as Ukraine strikes St. Petersburg
item.description: Ukrainian drone strikes on St. Petersburg have caused panic among Russia's business elite, who now directly witness how the Defense Ministry's actions affect their interests.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German prosecutors seek life sentence for Christmas market attacker
item.description: Prosecutors demanded life imprisonment for the Saudi man on trial over the 2024 car-ramming attack that killed six people at a German Christmas market.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU pledges €100M to strengthen Lebanese army amid ceasefire
item.description: The European Union approved a new 100-million-euro support package for Lebanon's military as it seeks to bolster the armed forces during a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah rejects US-brokered Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: Hezbollah rejected the latest US-brokered ceasefire agreement with Israel, which proposed pilot zones in southern Lebanon. Israel said it will continue operations and won't withdraw troops.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Crown Prince affirms solidarity with Bahrain after Iran attacks
item.description: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman held a phone call with Bahrain's King Hamad, expressing full solidarity and support following Iranian attacks on the Gulf state.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Former MI6 Chief Alex Younger Dies at 62
item.description: The former head of Britain's Secret Intelligence Service (2014-2020) died after a cancer diagnosis. Younger was one of the first publicly named spy chiefs and was praised by Prince William and PM Keir Starmer for his dedication to protecting the UK.

item.title: 🇨🇭 Switzerland Deploys 4,000 Troops for G7 Summit Security
item.description: Switzerland will deploy around 4,000 troops to reinforce security as world leaders including President Trump gather for the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains, France (June 15-17). The proximity to the Swiss border requires close coordination between civilian and military authorities.

item.title: ⚖️ Israel Supreme Court Rules ICRC Must Visit Palestinian Prisoners
item.description: Israel's Supreme Court unanimously rejected a government policy banning the Red Cross from visiting Palestinian detainees, ruling that blocking visits contravened Israeli and international law. The policy had been in place since October 2023.

item.title: 🏛️ John Bolton Reaches Plea Deal Over Mishandling Classified Info
item.description: Former National Security Adviser John Bolton has reached a plea agreement over mishandling sensitive national security information. Bolton, a prominent Trump critic, served in the administration before becoming one of its fiercest opponents.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Mystery Structure at Scarborough Reef Appears Then Vanishes
item.description: Satellite imagery shows a structure appeared in late May at Scarborough Reef in the South China Sea - a hotspot of China-Philippines tensions - but was later removed. The reef is claimed by both nations and has been a flashpoint for maritime disputes.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Rejects Lebanon Ceasefire, Dimming Prospects for Iran Deal
item.description: The Iran-backed group rejected a U.S.-brokered ceasefire with Israel and said it would continue fighting, undermining Trump administration efforts to de-escalate the broader conflict with Tehran.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu Faces Plummeting Support in Northern Israel as Voters Demand Tougher Lebanon Stance
item.description: A new poll shows Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu losing support in the electorally critical north where Hezbollah rocket fire has been heaviest, increasing pressure for a more aggressive approach.

item.title: 📈 Gulf Markets Mixed as Lebanon Ceasefire Fuels Iran Deal Optimism
item.description: Stock markets across the Gulf ended mixed after Israel and Lebanon agreed to implement a ceasefire, with investors hopeful for a broader resolution to the U.S.-Iran conflict.

item.title: 💵 Dollar Slips from Two-Month High as Lebanon Ceasefire Eases Tensions
item.description: The dollar retreated from a two-month high on optimism about a ceasefire in Lebanon, while the yen hovered near the 160 level where intervention risks remain elevated.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuban Journalist Recounts Harassment After Launching Independent Magazine
item.description: The founder of Cuba first independent magazine describes being isolated, monitored and interrogated by state security after attempting to tell Cuban stories without government interference.

item.title: 🇳🇱🇺🇦⚔️ Dutch Combat Robot Plant Opens New Supply Pipeline for Ukraine
item.description: A new Dutch facility producing THeMIS unmanned ground vehicles for combat zones has delivered its first unit, with over 100 vehicles planned to support Ukrainian forces.

item.title: 🇷🇺🛢️💥 Russia Admits Oil Output Falling as Ukrainian Drone Strikes Hit Refineries
item.description: Deputy PM Novak confirmed declining crude production citing unscheduled repairs at refineries damaged by Ukrainian drone attacks, acknowledging sanctions and drone warfare impact.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱⚔️ Hezbollah Drone Struck IDF Northern Commander's Car in Lebanon Visit
item.description: A Hezbollah drone attack targeted IDF Northern Commander Rafi Milo's vehicle during a recent Lebanon visit, an attack that would have been a major strategic victory if successful.

item.title: 🇸🇦🇧🇭🇮🇷 Saudi Crown Prince Condemns Iranian Attacks on Bahrain
item.description: MbS expressed solidarity with Bahrain during a call with King Hamad, condemning Iranian attacks against the Gulf state amid escalating regional tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine strikes kill four in occupied Crimea, fuel shortages spread
item.description: Russian-backed officials report four killed in Ukrainian attacks on Simferopol and a commuter train near Kerch. Ukraine's drone campaign has caused fuel rationing across the peninsula, with strikes also hitting near St Petersburg.

item.title: 🇸🇴 Heavy gunfire in Mogadishu as Somalia election dispute escalates
item.description: Government forces and opposition fighters exchanged fire in the capital after the president extended his term by one year. The opposition called the extension unconstitutional and protesters were met with violence.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel approves 2,162 new settlement homes in occupied West Bank
item.description: Far-right Finance Minister Smotrich announced the expansion, saying it would prevent a Palestinian state. The move was condemned by the Palestinian Authority and is illegal under international law.

item.title: 🚀 NASA declares Mars Maven spacecraft dead after six months of silence
item.description: The spacecraft, which studied Mars' atmosphere for over a decade, went silent in December after spinning out of control. Four other orbiters will take over its relay duties for surface rovers.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇷🇺 French jets intercept Russian aircraft 11 times over the Baltics in one week
item.description: French warplanes were scrambled 11 times as part of NATO Baltic Air Policing Mission, with Frances armed forces spokesperson describing higher-than-usual Russian provocations amid ongoing Ukraine war tensions.

item.title: 🇸🇪🇪🇺 Sweden urges EU to tighten tourist visa rules for Russians
item.description: Swedens migration minister called for stricter EU rules on Russian tourist visas, saying it was insane that many Russians can still travel freely despite the ongoing war in Ukraine.
